channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: As Trump threatens mass deportations, Central America braces for an influx of vulnerable migrants
item.description: SAN PEDRO SULA, Honduras (AP) — As dozens of deported migrants pack into a sweltering airport facility in San Pedro Sula, Norma sits under fluorescent lights clutching a foam cup of coffee and a small plate of eggs – all that was waiting for her in Honduras.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from Indonesian firing squad prepares to fly home
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina who was on death row in Indonesia and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 will return home this week under an arrangement between the countries, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI and NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s impeached leader avoids investigators as court begins meeting to determine his fate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol dodged requests by investigative agencies to appear for questioning over his short-lived martial law decree, as the Constitutional Court began its first meeting Monday on Yoon's case to determine whether to formally unseat or
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For the first time in 8 years, a US Navy ship makes a port call in Cambodia, a top Chinese ally
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — A U.S. Navy warship arrived Monday in Cambodia, the first such visit in eight years to a nation that is China’s close ally in Southeast Asia. Cambodia's government has suggested the port call reflects an upgrade in often-strained relations.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's Scholz faces a confidence vote. It's expected to lead to an election in February
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz faces a confidence vote in the German parliament on Monday that he's expected to lose, paving the way for the European Union's most populous member and biggest economy to hold an early election in February.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel closes its embassy in Ireland
item.description: Israel announced that it would close its embassy in Ireland on Sunday after the European country recognized Palestine as a sovereign nation and other acts that Israel said promoted “antisemitic rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Bolivia's divisive popular vote for senior judges offers lessons for Mexico
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning in Bolivia for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of the capital La Paz and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into off
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Christians in Syria mark country's transformation with tears as UN envoy urges an end to sanctions
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — In churches across long-stifled Syria, Christians marked the first Sunday services since the collapse of Bashar Assad 's government in an air of transformation. Some were in tears. Others clasped their hands in prayer.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is 'several hundred,' top official says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The death toll in the French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is “several hundred” and may run into the thousands, the island’s top government official told a local broadcaster Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: Israel says it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorate over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials say new Israeli airstrikes have killed over 46 people including children.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Natalie Melzer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Activists release images of foxes at Finnish fur farms to push EU to ban the trade
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — A red fox frantically scratches the wires of its small cage. An Arctic fox meanders lazily with a bloody tail. Other furry creatures, some with teary eyes, stare blankly into a light on an activist's video camera.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken confirms US contact with Syrian rebel group
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said that U.S. officials have been in "direct contact" with Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), the Syrian opposition group that effectively led to the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime in the country, the Associated Press reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: South Korea’s president impeached over brief martial law decree
item.description: South Korea's parliament voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol early Saturday morning, after a brief decree of martial law thrust the country into temporary political peril. The motion to impeach Yoon passed with a 204-85 vote, The Associated Press (AP) reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A look at the whirlwind events that led to the impeachment of South Korea's president
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday voted to impeach embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law decree this month, a shocking stunt that paralyzed the country’s politics, halted foreign policy and spooked financial markets.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia batters Ukraine power grid amid rising concern Putin could order ballistic missile attack this weekend
item.description: Ukraine's energy infrastructure was again pummeled in a "massive blow" by Russian missiles and drones on Friday, just days after the U.S. warned Russia might be planning another Oreshnik missile strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Syrian prison official charged in US with torture
item.description: A former Syrian official under the rule of former President Bashar Assad was federally indicted in the U.S. for torturing prisoners held at Damascus Central Prison from 2005-08, according to a Thursday release from the Justice Department. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France's Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Top US ally, SDF commander in Syria warns of ISIS return if Turkish airstrikes don’t stop
item.description: Gen. Mazloum Abdi, the commander of the Syrian Democratic Forces, said the Turkish military continue to attack his Kurdish forces, despite a U.S. brokered ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Travis Timmerman, the missing American apparently found in Syria
item.description: An American man found wandering the streets outside the Syrian capital of Damascus barefoot identified himself as Pete Travis Timmerman on Thursday. Timmerman was last seen in Budapest, Hungary, in June and had not been heard from in months.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Biden readies travel bans on Georgia officials for undermining October election
item.description: President Biden is readying visa restrictions on officials in the country of Georgia amid a government crackdown on mass protests against the claimed victory by the Georgian Dream party, which critics say is turning the country toward Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump has invited Xi Jinping to attend inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month in Washington, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: American who was detained by Assad regime while on Christian pilgrimage freed
item.description: Travis Timmerman appeared free in Syria on Thursday, saying he was detained by the Assad regime seven months ago after crossing into the country during a Christian pilgrimage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken testifies at hearing on Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Lawmakers have been demanding answers into how such a significant operation could have gone so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
